title: HEXplore It: Opinion Evolved: Or, Never Be Afraid to Change Your Mind
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=a2L6AbyANjw

Daniel (@dungeondive) takes a fascinating journey from skepticism to love with HEXplore It: The Forests of Adrimon. Initially put off by the game's awkward name, colorless art, and amateurish card design, he almost never gave it a shot—until Tom Chick's glowing review inspired a second look. What he discovered beneath that unglamorous presentation is a genuinely exceptional hex-crawl adventure game brimming with thematic depth and engaging exploration mechanics. The game brilliantly combines procedural exploration with quest-driven narratives, creating compelling short stories as you hunt for artifact fragments and face off against an evil sorceress. Daniel's candid acknowledgment of his initial prejudices serves as a powerful reminder: sometimes the games worth our time hide behind unconventional packaging.

Whether you've been sleeping on HEXplore It or are already a devoted fan, this is a masterclass in being open-minded about board games. Daniel doesn't shy away from his criticisms—the bare iconography really does feel like placeholder art—but he shows how an exceptional game design can transcend visual limitations through imagination and engagement.

**What initially held you back from trying a game you later fell in love with, and what finally convinced you to give it a chance?**

----
